name: Pineapple Tart
recipeIngredient: four 17 by 12-inch phyllo sheets - thawed if frozen, stacked between two sheets of wax paper and covered with a kitchen towel
1 large ripe pineapple (about 4 1/2 pounds), peeled, halved lengthwise
cored and cut crosswise into 1/4-inch-thick slices
seeds scraped from 1/2 vanilla bean - halved lengthwise, and pod reserved
recipeInstructions:
Make the shell:
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and lightly coat an 11-inch tart pan with vegetable oil cooking spray.
In a food processor, grind graham crackers with sugar to fine crumbs. Working quickly, line the tart pan with one phyllo sheet, allowing edges to overhang evenly, and with a pastry brush dot the phyllo on the bottom of the tart pan with butter in 4 or 5 places. Sprinkle phyllo with one third of the crumb mixture and top with another phyllo sheet, arranging it at right angles to the first phyllo sheet so overhang is even all around. Dot phyllo with butter and sprinkle with one third of the crumb mixture in the same manner. Repeat layering procedure with remaining two phyllo sheets, the remaining one third of the crumb mixture, and butter in the same manner, ending with phyllo. Trim phyllo overhang with scissors to one inch beyond the rim of the tart pan and roll toward the center, forming a shallow edge just inside the tart pan. Brush shell with remaining butter and bake in the middle of the oven for 12 minutes, or until golden and crisp. Cool shell in the tart pan on a rack.
Shell may be made up to one day ahead and kept, covered loosely, in the tart pan in a cool dry place.
Make the filling:
In a heavy 12-inch skillet, simmer pineapple, sugars, vanilla seeds, and reserved vanilla pod over moderate heat, carefully turning pineapple occasionally (trying to keep slices whole), until most liquid is evaporated and pineapple is slightly translucent (reducing heat as liquid is evaporated), about 25 minutes. Cool mixture slightly and discard vanilla pod.
Arrange pineapple in overlapping concentric circles in the shell and top with any remaining syrup in the skillet.
Tart may be made up to one day ahead and kept, covered loosely, at room temperature.
Each serving contains approximately 180 calories and 4 grams of fat (19% of calories from fat).
Yield: 8 servings
